plan_metadata.name: Feature Verify Project Type Selection Shows Only DA CEA Teams

steps.description: Use the keyboard shortcut **Ctrl + `** to open the integrated terminal in the Visual Studio Code workspace welcome screen.
Type the text `open https://dev.teams.microsoft.com` into the integrated terminal (bash) at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code workspace.
Press the Enter key in the integrated Terminal of Visual Studio Code to execute the command: open https://dev.teams.microsoft.com.
Enter "${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}" into the email or username input field on the Microsoft "Sign in" page at login.microsoftonline.com.
Click the "Next" button on the Microsoft login page to proceed with the sign-in process after entering an email address.
Click on the "Password" input field on the Microsoft login page to focus the cursor for entering a password.
Type the password '${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}}' into the "Enter password" field on the Microsoft account sign-in page for user in a Chrome browser window.
Press the Enter key in the password input field on the Microsoft login page to submit the entered password for the user.
Click the "No" button in the Microsoft "Stay signed in?" dialog on the login.microsoftonline.com sign-in page.
Reduce the zoom level in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web interface by pressing Ctrl and minus (-) while viewing the "App features" section for the app.

Zoom out the "App features" page in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal by pressing the keyboard shortcut Ctrl + -; this reduces the UI scale and provides a wider view of feature options being configured for the app.

Use keyboard shortcut Ctrl + - to zoom out the Dashboard view in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web application, making UI elements in the "Dashboard" and left sidebar (Overview, Configure, Advanced, Develop sections) appear smaller for broader visibility.
Click the "Open in Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" option in the left sidebar under the Develop section of the Developer Portal app interface.
Click the "Open in Visual Studio Code (JS/TS)" button within the Develop section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web app.
Click the "Open Visual Studio Code URL Handler" button in the browser popup dialog requesting permission to open Visual Studio Code from the Developer Portal application.
Click the close button (red "X") located at the top-right corner of the "Welcome to VS Code" sign-in overlay to dismiss the dialog.
Click the "Open" button in the confirmation dialog prompting to allow the 'Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit' extension to open a URI in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft 365 login prompt within the Visual Studio Code interface, located at the top center of the screen.
Click the "Use weaker encryption" button in the OS keyring warning dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Password" input field on the Microsoft login screen in a web browser to activate text entry for account authentication.
Type text "${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}}" into the password field on the Microsoft login page for user.
Click the blue "Sign in" button on the Microsoft login page to submit the entered password and proceed with authentication.
Click the browser window's "Close" button (red X) in the top-right corner to exit the "Visual Studio Code – You are signed in now and can close this page" tab.
@assertion "Declarative Agent", "Custom Engine Agent" and "Teams Agents and Apps" exist. "Graph Connector" and "Office Add-in" are not shown.

@assertion "Declarative Agent" includes description "Create your own agent by declaring instructions". "Custom Engine Agent" includes description "Build intelligent agent where you manage orchestration and provide your own LLM". "Teams Agents and Apps" includes description "Create agent, chat bot, web pages or other apps in Microsoft".
